description:
Goal
Make native lowering and codegen use demand-shaped callable operands so direct calls do not allocate or publish first-class closures.

Problem being fixed
src/compiler2/jobs/native.rs currently materializes every BackendStep::FunctionRef/Lambda with MakeFnRef/MakeClosure, selects callable boundaries by capture types, and scans all callable-producing steps as identity needs. That imports old-world behavior too broadly and overallocates when the callable is only used directly.

Code seams
- src/compiler2/jobs/native.rs: lowering of BackendStep::FunctionRef/Lambda, settled_callable_boundary, collect_callable_identity_needs, entry_signature, bind_entry_input, entry_call_args_from_value, delivered_args_for_abi.
- src/compiler2/native_codegen/*: callable boundary signatures and direct closure call emission.

Algorithm
1. Lower DirectCallable operands as executable target plus demanded captures and surface inputs. No MakeClosure/MakeFnRef is emitted for direct-only demand.
2. Lower FirstClassCallable operands through the runtime callable object path and publish/use callable entries as boundary contracts.
3. Internal executable calls should use the executable ABI derived from runtime input layout. Callable-entry ABI remains only for externally callable first-class values.
4. Capture/context placement must be a deliberate ABI choice, not inherited from old-world closure shape. Prefer an internal signature that keeps user inputs first and appends closure/capture context only when a direct callable target requires it.
5. Codegen should receive already-settled callable/capture information; it should not select boundaries by re-reading capture types.

Removal target
Delete settled_callable_boundary capture-type selection if demand-derived callable boundary IDs make it obsolete. Delete callable identity collection from all backend steps; identities should come from demanded first-class entries and direct-call targets only.

Checks
- Native JIT/AOT tests pass for direct lambda call, escaped lambda, opaque callable, enum reducer cases, and quicksort.
- --log-telemetry confirms direct-only callable demand does not emit MakeClosure/MakeFnRef or callable_entries.
- No native code path publishes callable boundaries from undemanded lambda/function-ref steps.
title: Compiler2 native: lower direct callable demand without first-class closure materialization
